---
title: "Privacy Law Roundup — July 16, 2026"
date: 2026-07-16 10:54:07 +0530
categories: [news, roundup]
tags: [dpdpa, india, privacy, global, news]
---

## India: Regulatory Updates

India's IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed that the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Rules have been finalized and are slated for publication in the coming days. This announcement marks a significant step towards the full implementation of the DPDPA, 2023. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMisAFBVV95cUxQakx6Y1RYS0lCdGEzN1VfZENmenhLRVZRWUViVUNwZHRCZzhZbDJFYkN4UUdOb1NKZzFKUHdIQnRxWDVINnhTaWY5Ml9IUFppTW44ZkVZeTFzTndwaUo2bl9GYmpHU3U4dkV5UEtGbHdXbG93OGs4QXBSQ3I5Ums5SDFGREdYRHdJQXp2YkpzcTVHaC0wLV9qaDlRRnp2Y25obXhIQVdjSHhsbjZFa0wwTA?oc=5))

The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has initiated the process to establish the Data Protection Board of India, inviting applications for its chairperson and members. This move is crucial for setting up the independent body responsible for enforcing the DPDPA. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiyAFBVV95cUxOaVR4MW9NWUdLSkM5eEVJNFlNeWJBVVRRNzZaU3d0VGdlMlh0V0ExNlNrU2lUdllVMGpYdFFGQ3lIZ1kyOEFIbXNlWks2cHFSX2lTRlB4d1gxY1NDbU1FV3NfRFVYYTB2V29haVpVTEYyWmtDeHFHLURaYnZyaUtlTlptazN3dTc0cVVIajJoenhPTEVCRTFLZjlfVW94OU1YT0U5RWV2VjdZNnJULUlqd1ViM2RBQm02Ti1GTVpWc1hPNnUwZk5XdA?oc=5))

## India: Litigation & Legal Analysis

The Supreme Court has issued notice regarding a Public Interest Litigation that challenges the constitutionality of both the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and the recently finalized DPDP Rules, 2025. This indicates potential judicial scrutiny of the new privacy framework. ([Source](https://news.google.?oc=5))

In a separate development, the Supreme Court has directed MeitY to examine a PIL that seeks the recovery or destruction of Indian citizens' stolen personal data held on foreign servers. This highlights growing concerns over data breaches and the extraterritorial reach of data protection efforts. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-?oc=5))

Legal experts are debating the potential interpretational uncertainty in privacy adjudication due to the DPDPA's lack of a statutory definition for "harm." This absence could lead to varied judicial interpretations in future cases. ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxOZHFHUHJTc0pzTzY4QmlBMS1zVWE5LVlFeTc2RV9VQk9yUWd6c25PbFlVa2owWGsyZzUzZVRlV2lvTkt0NHg1eC03NUd4eFFEWDJtZWdfQjhqdEt4SDFtaU5NSkx0UjJ6ZUhNdllKR0V2RzV1UEhXUG5ySFVub3MwYlNBWXBtWlJhMzl3NnBzbUdlR1
